title: T-024 — Wiring: Allergen cascade rule deployment to rule_definitions
prompt:
# T-024 — Wiring: Allergen cascade rule deployment to rule_definitions

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §10.2, §10.3, §10.8
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Deploy cascade rule definition + handler; assert RM→intermediate→FG propagation and override protection.

Rule row written via Drizzle migration that targets rule_definitions table (created by 02-SETTINGS). Handler executes: SELECT distinct active BOMs containing changed component; aggregate allergens; UPSERT cascaded rows; never overwrite manual_override. End-to-end test seeds a small graph FG -> intermediate -> RM and asserts propagation under <5s. Note: `item_type` is on `items`, not `item_allergen_profiles`; join through item_id when detecting raw-material profile changes.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_allergen_cascade_rule.sql [create]
- lib/technical/allergens/cascade.ts [create]
- tests/wiring/allergen-cascade.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Cascade preview UI (T-047)
- Allergen changeover gate in 08-PRODUCTION

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given FG has BOM with RM that gets allergen A01 added, when cascade runs, then FG profile contains cascaded A01 within one engine cycle.
2. Given FG has manual_override row for A01='trace', when RM A01 changes intensity, then FG manual_override row is preserved unchanged.
3. Given KPI target ≤5s, when measured in test, then propagation completes within 5000ms.
4. Given manufacturing_operation has allergen_addition for A12, when BOM uses that op, then FG cascaded set includes A12.
5. Given item_allergen_profiles has no item_type column, when trigger/change detection runs, then it joins items by item_id to filter raw-material changes.
6. UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

## Test strategy
- RED tests covering propagation + override protection + timing.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/wiring/allergen-cascade.test.ts
- For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.

## Risk red lines
- Never overwrite source='manual_override' rows.
- Do not run cascade synchronously in API request paths.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
